info.title: Omnipath
info.description: Multi-agent AI system with observability, meta-learning, and agent economy

get.description: Health check endpoint for monitoring and load balancers
Get version information
Root endpoint with API information

post.description:
Register a new user

Creates a new user account with email and password.

User login

Authenticates user and returns access and refresh tokens.

Refresh access token

Uses refresh token to get a new access token.

User logout

Revokes the current access token.

get.description:
Get current user info

Returns the authenticated user's information.

Verify token validity

Returns 200 if token is valid, 401 otherwise.

post.description:
Create a new tenant

Creates a new tenant/organization in the system with database persistence.

get.description:
List all tenants

Returns a list of tenants from the database with optional filtering.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Max number of records to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Max number of records to return

schema.title: Response List Tenants Api V1 Tenants Get

get.description:
Get tenant by ID

Retrieves a specific tenant's information from the database.

put.description:
Update tenant

Updates a tenant's information in the database.

delete.description:
Delete tenant

Deletes a tenant from the database.
Note: This will cascade delete all related users, agents, and missions.

post.description:
Create a new agent

Creates a new AI agent with specified configuration.
Agent will be created under the authenticated user's tenant.

get.description:
List all agents

Returns a list of agents with optional filtering.
Users can only see agents in their own tenant.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Max number of records to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Max number of records to return

schema.title: Response List Agents Api V1 Agents Get

get.description:
Get agent by ID

Retrieves a specific agent's information.
Users can only access agents in their own tenant.

put.description:
Update agent

Updates an agent's configuration.
Users can only update agents in their own tenant.

delete.description:
Delete agent

Deletes an agent from the system.
Users can only delete agents in their own tenant.

post.description:
Activate agent

Sets agent status to IDLE (ready to accept missions).
Users can only activate agents in their own tenant.

Deactivate agent

Sets agent status to OFFLINE (not accepting missions).
Users can only deactivate agents in their own tenant.

get.description:
List available specialized agent types

Returns information about all specialized agent types including their
capabilities, tools, and use cases.

This endpoint does not require any parameters and is available to all
authenticated users.

schema.title: Response List Specialized Agent Types Api V1 Agents Types Get

post.description:
Execute a task with a specialized agent

This endpoint allows direct execution of specialized agents (Researcher, Analyst, Developer)
with their reasoning workflows and tool-calling capabilities.

The agent will use LangGraph-based reasoning to plan, execute, reflect, and adapt
its approach to complete the task.

**Agent Types:**
- `researcher`: Conducts research, gathers information, synthesizes findings
- `analyst`: Analyzes data, performs calculations, generates insights
- `developer`: Generates code, debugs issues, writes tests

**Example Tasks:**
- Researcher: `{"query": "What is quantum computing?", "depth": "standard"}`
- Analyst: `{"data": {...}, "analysis_type": "descriptive"}`
- Developer: `{"task_type": "generate", "specification": "Create a sorting function"}`

get.description:
List all available tools for agents

Returns information about all tools that specialized agents can use,
including their descriptions, categories, and usage examples.

schema.title: Response List Available Tools Api V1 Agents Tools Get

post.description:
Execute a research task with the Researcher agent

Convenience endpoint for directly executing research tasks without
needing to construct the full request payload.

The Researcher agent will:
1. Search the web for relevant information
2. Synthesize findings from multiple sources
3. Provide citations and sources
4. Generate a comprehensive research report

**Parameters:**
- `query`: The research question or topic
- `depth`: Research depth - "standard" for quick research, "deep" for comprehensive

**Example:**
```
POST /api/v1/agents/research
{
    "query": "What is the latest research on quantum computing?",
    "depth": "deep"
}
```

Execute an analysis task with the Analyst agent

Convenience endpoint for directly executing data analysis tasks.

The Analyst agent will:
1. Analyze the provided data
2. Perform statistical calculations
3. Identify patterns and trends
4. Generate actionable insights

**Parameters:**
- `data`: The data to analyze (dict, list, or structured data)
- `analysis_type`: Type of analysis - "descriptive", "comparative", "predictive"

**Example:**
```
POST /api/v1/agents/analyze
{
    "data": {"sales": [100, 200, 150, 300], "costs": [50, 80, 60, 120]},
    "analysis_type": "descriptive"
}
```

Execute a development task with the Developer agent

Convenience endpoint for directly executing code-related tasks.

The Developer agent will:
1. Generate code based on specifications
2. Debug and fix code issues
3. Write unit tests
4. Review code and provide feedback

**Parameters:**
- `specification`: Description of what code to generate or task to perform
- `task_type`: Type of task - "generate", "debug", "review", "test"

**Example:**
```
POST /api/v1/agents/develop
{
    "specification": "Create a function to calculate the fibonacci sequence",
    "task_type": "generate"
}
```

Create a new mission

Creates a new mission for an agent to execute.
Mission will be created under the authenticated user's tenant.
Agent must belong to the same tenant.

get.description:
List all missions

Returns a list of missions with optional filtering.
Users can only see missions in their own tenant.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Max number of records to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Max number of records to return

schema.title: Response List Missions Api V1 Missions Get

get.description:
Get mission by ID

Retrieves a specific mission's information.
Users can only access missions in their own tenant.

put.description:
Update mission

Updates a mission's status or result.
Users can only update missions in their own tenant.

delete.description:
Delete mission

Deletes a mission from the system.
Users can only delete missions in their own tenant.

post.description:
Start mission execution

Begins executing a pending mission.
Users can only start missions in their own tenant.

Complete mission execution

Marks a mission as completed with results.
Users can only complete missions in their own tenant.

Fail mission execution

Marks a mission as failed with error details.
Users can only fail missions in their own tenant.

Cancel mission execution

Cancels a pending or running mission.
Users can only cancel missions in their own tenant.

get.description: Get all agent balances for the current tenant

schema.title: Response Get Agent Balances Api V1 Economy Balance Get

get.description: Get transaction history for the tenant or specific agent

schema.title: Response Get Transactions Api V1 Economy Transactions Get

post.description:
Add credits to the tenant's economy
In production, this would integrate with a payment processor

get.description: Get economy statistics for the tenant

Get performance metrics for all agents in the tenant.

Returns success rates, costs, and self-improvement activity computed
directly from the missions table.

schema.title: Response Get All Agent Performance Api V1 Performance Agents Get

get.description:
Get performance metrics for a specific agent.

Raises 404 if the agent does not exist or belongs to a different tenant.

Get self-improvement event history.

Improvement events are stored in ``agent.config["improvements"]``.
Returns an empty list if no improvements have been recorded.

schema.title: Response Get Improvement History Api V1 Performance Improvements Get

get.description:
Get performance trends over time for an agent.

Aggregates mission data by calendar day over the requested window and
returns one data point per day that had at least one mission.

Raises 404 if the agent does not exist or belongs to a different tenant.

schema.title: Response Get Performance Trends Api V1 Performance Trends  Agent Id  Get

post.description:
Manually trigger self-improvement analysis for an agent.

Normally happens automatically every 10 missions.  This endpoint queues
an immediate analysis regardless of the mission count threshold.

get.description:
Prometheus metrics endpoint

Exposes all Prometheus metrics for scraping

Get performance metrics for a specific agent

Returns aggregated statistics including:
- Success rate
- Average cost and duration
- Quality scores
- Complexity breakdown
- Recent trends

Get performance metrics for all agents

Returns a dictionary of agent_id -> performance metrics

Get top performing agents by a specific metric

Available metrics:
- success_rate: Percentage of successful missions
- avg_cost_per_mission: Average cost efficiency
- avg_duration_per_mission: Average speed
- average_quality: Average output quality

Get actionable learning insights for an agent

Provides:
- Performance summary
- Identified patterns
- Recommendations for improvement
- Best practices for this agent

Comprehensive analysis of an agent's performance

Includes:
- Model performance analysis
- Complexity analysis
- Cost efficiency analysis
- Quality analysis
- Optimization recommendations

Get optimization recommendations for an agent

Returns specific, actionable recommendations with:
- Current vs recommended values
- Expected improvement
- Confidence level
- Reasoning

post.description:
Automatically generate optimized configuration for an agent

Analyzes performance data and creates an optimized configuration
including:
- Preferred model
- Optimal complexity level
- Cost limits
- Quality thresholds

get.description:
Get the current optimized configuration for an agent

Returns the configuration generated by auto-optimization

Get insights about mission complexity across all agents

Shows success rates, costs, and durations for each complexity level

Get system-wide insights across all agents

Provides:
- Overall statistics
- Top performers
- System-wide trends
- Complexity analysis

Get recent mission outcomes

Useful for debugging and monitoring recent performance

Get overall meta-learning system statistics

Quick overview of the learning system status

post.description:
Record a mission outcome for learning

This endpoint is typically called by the mission executor
after a mission completes.

Expected payload:
{
    "mission_id": "string",
    "agent_id": "string",
    "tenant_id": "string",
    "outcome": "success|failure|partial|error",
    "duration_seconds": float,
    "cost": float,
    "complexity": "simple|moderate|complex|swarm",
    "model_used": "string (optional)",
    "tokens_used": int (optional),
    "quality_score": float (optional),
    "error_message": "string (optional)"
}

delete.description:
Clear all performance tracking data

⚠️ WARNING: This will delete all learning data!
Use only for testing or reset purposes.

post.description:
Register a new AI asset.

Creates a new asset in the registry with the specified properties.
All agents, tools, and models must be registered before use.

get.description:
List assets with optional filters.

Returns a list of assets matching the specified filters.
If no filters are provided, returns all assets.

schema.title: Response List Assets Api V1 Registry Assets Get

get.description:
Get an asset by ID.

Returns the asset details including lineage, metadata, and dependencies.

put.description:
Update an asset.

Updates the specified fields of an asset. Only provided fields are updated.

delete.description:
Delete an asset.

Removes the asset from the registry. This action cannot be undone.

get.description:
Get dependencies for an asset.

Returns the list of assets that this asset depends on.
If recursive=true, returns all transitive dependencies.

schema.title: Response Get Dependencies Api V1 Registry Assets  Asset Id  Dependencies Get

get.description:
Get assets that depend on this asset.

Returns the list of assets that have this asset as a dependency.

schema.title: Response Get Dependents Api V1 Registry Assets  Asset Id  Dependents Get

get.description:
Get lineage for an asset.

Returns the model lineage information including base model,
fine-tuning data, and vector database sources.

schema.title: Response Get Lineage Api V1 Registry Assets  Asset Id  Lineage Get

get.description:
Get the full lineage chain for an asset.

Traces back through dependencies to find the origin.
Returns the chain from origin to current asset.

schema.title: Response Get Lineage Chain Api V1 Registry Assets  Asset Id  Lineage Chain Get

get.description:
Get lineage events for an asset.

Returns the timeline of events (created, fine-tuned, updated, deprecated)
for the specified asset, sorted by timestamp (newest first).

schema.title: Response Get Lineage Events Api V1 Registry Assets  Asset Id  Events Get

post.description:
Track a lineage event.

Creates a new event in the lineage timeline for an asset.

get.description:

schema.title: Response Get Registry Stats Api V1 Registry Stats Get

get.description: Get a specific tag definition by name.

delete.description:

Note: This only removes the auto-tagging rule, not existing tags on assets.

post.description:
Apply contextual tags to an asset based on usage context.

Analyzes the provided context and automatically applies relevant tags.

get.description: Get all contextual tags for an asset with history.

schema.title: Response Get Asset Tags Api V1 Tags Assets  Asset Id  Tags Get

delete.description: Remove a specific tag from an asset.

post.description:
Assess risk level for an asset based on its tags.

Maps asset tags to EU AI Act risk categories and generates compliance requirements.

get.description: Get current risk assessment for an asset.

post.description:
Check if user has authority to access an asset based on risk level.

Enforces risk-based access control using authority levels.

get.description: Get all assets that have a specific tag.
Get overall tag usage statistics.
Get compliance report showing assets by risk level and requirements.

post.description:
Calculate risk score for an asset.

This endpoint calculates a comprehensive risk score based on:
- Inherent risk (EU AI Act level)
- Data sensitivity (tags)
- Operational context (production, user-facing, etc.)
- Historical risk (past incidents)

Returns:
    Risk score with breakdown by factor

get.description:
Get current risk score for an asset (from cache if available).

Returns cached score if available and not expired, otherwise calculates new score.

Returns:
    Risk score with breakdown

Get detailed risk breakdown by factor.

Returns:
    Dictionary mapping risk factors to scores

schema.title: Response Get Risk Breakdown Api V1 Risk Assets  Asset Id  Breakdown Get

post.description:
Recalculate risk scores for all assets.

This is a maintenance endpoint for bulk recalculation.

Returns:
    List of updated risk scores

schema.title: Response Recalculate All Scores Api V1 Risk Recalculate All Post

Assess comprehensive impact for an asset.

Evaluates three dimensions:
- Business impact (revenue, customers, penalties, reputation)
